findings.observation: Breadcrumb shows raw truncated UUID (ee000000-0000-0000-0000...) instead of the CVE ID (CVE-2025-1972). Visible across all detail page tabs (screenshots 15-19). This is the most visible enterprise-readiness failure.
findings.suggestion: Replace UUID in breadcrumb with the CVE ID string. Breadcrumb should read 'CVEs / CVE-2025-1972'. Never expose internal database identifiers.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > No Debug Artifacts; enterprise-override > Professional Appearance

findings.element: Pagination footer on filtered view (High filter)
findings.observation: Pagination shows 'Showing 1-25 of 14 CVEs' when High filter is active. Range (1-25) exceeds total (14). Makes pagination look broken and erodes trust in data accuracy.
findings.suggestion: Clamp the range to actual row count: 'Showing 1-14 of 14 CVEs'. Page-size (25) must not appear in range when fewer items than page size.

findings.observation: No h1 heading on the CVEs listing page. 'CVEs' appears as text in topbar but not as semantic h1. Confirmed by assertion BUILTIN-A11Y-003. Every other listing page has an h1.
findings.suggestion: Wrap 'CVEs' page title in an h1 element (22px, font-600, --text-emphasis).
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Heading Hierarchy; consistency-override > Page Type 1 section 1.1

findings.observation: Stat cards show static unfiltered counts regardless of active filters. When Critical filter is applied (6 rows shown), stat cards still display 62/6/14/28/11. Creates disconnect between summary metrics and displayed data.
findings.suggestion: Either update stat card values to reflect filtered dataset, or show 'Showing X of Y (filtered from Z total)' in pagination footer to make the relationship explicit.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals; interaction-override > Filtering

findings.element: Stat card row — missing Low severity card
findings.observation: Stat cards show Total(62)/Critical(6)/High(14)/Medium(28)/KEV(11) but omit Low severity entirely. The pill filters show 'Low 14' so the data exists. Math: 6+14+28+14=62, but stat cards show 6+14+28+11=59. Mixes severity breakdown with cross-cutting dimension (KEV).
findings.suggestion: Add a Low stat card to maintain severity decomposition. Move KEV to a separate indicator or add as 6th card. Stat cards must decompose into the total.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals; consistency-override > Stat Card Anatomy

findings.element: Empty state — date range 'Last 30 Days' filter
findings.observation: Selecting 'Last 30 Days' returns empty table with just column headers and faint 'No CVEs found' text. No icon, no explanation, no 'Clear filters' CTA. Stat cards above still show 62 total — looks broken, not legitimately empty.
findings.suggestion: Use EmptyState component: filter icon, 'No CVEs in this date range' title, 'No CVEs published in the last 30 days. Try expanding the date range.' subtitle, 'Clear Filters' CTA button.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; consistency-override > Empty Table State

findings.observation: Searching for non-existent term shows bare 'No CVEs found.' text centered in table with no icon, no subtext, no CTA. Footer repeats 'No CVEs found'. Looks like broken state rather than intentional.
findings.suggestion: Use EmptyState component: search icon, 'No CVEs found' title, 'Try adjusting your search terms or clearing filters' subtitle, 'Clear Search' CTA.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; consistency-override > Empty Table State

findings.element: Pagination footer when filters are active
findings.observation: When filtered, pagination shows 'Showing 1-6 of 6 CVEs' with no indication of unfiltered total. User loses context about how many were filtered out.
findings.suggestion: Show 'Showing 1-6 of 6 CVEs (filtered from 62 total)' when any filter is active.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals; consistency-override > Pagination Footer

findings.element: Pagination total count vs stat card total
findings.observation: Unfiltered pagination shows 'Showing 1-25 of 56 CVEs' but stat cards show '62 TOTAL'. A 6-CVE discrepancy on the same page with no filters active. Destroys data credibility.
findings.suggestion: Investigate query discrepancy. Stat cards and pagination must show same total when unfiltered. If stat cards include a broader scope, clarify with labels.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals; data-integrity.md > Data sourced from same query

findings.element: CVEs listing page — no Export button
findings.observation: No export/CSV functionality on the CVEs listing page. Patches page has an Export button. Enterprise security teams need CVE data for compliance reports, GRC tools, and board presentations.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Export' button (ghost/outline style) in page header. Support CSV export of currently filtered CVE data. Include all visible columns plus description.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Print and Export; consistency.md > Action Patterns

findings.observation: Exploit 'Yes' displayed in green (signal-healthy). Having an exploit available is a DANGER signal, not healthy. Green = safe per the design system. This semantically misleads users into thinking exploit availability is a positive indicator.
findings.suggestion: Use --signal-critical (red) for Exploit 'Yes'. Use --signal-warning (amber) for KEV 'Yes'. Show em dash for absent values. Add 6px colored dot before text per status badge pattern.
findings.standard_violated: visual-override > Signal Colors; consistency-override > Status Badge Pattern; accessibility.md > Color as Sole Differentiator

findings.observation: At 375px, sidebar takes 60% of screen, stat cards/filters occupy rest, data table is invisible. Page is effectively broken with no 'best viewed on desktop' message.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Best viewed on desktop' banner for viewports below 1024px. Collapse sidebar behind hamburger menu. Ensure table is at minimum scrollable.

findings.observation: Card view cards lack consistent anatomy: no clear header/body/footer separation, missing key data (exploit status, KEV, endpoints count). Action buttons ('Snooze', 'Dismiss') exist in cards but not in table — feature parity gap. Cards under 300px minimum width.
findings.suggestion: Restructure cards: Header (CVE ID + severity badge), Body (CVSS gauge + exploit/KEV/endpoints), Footer (action buttons with border-top). Min 300px width. Sync actions with table kebab menu.

findings.element: 50+ icon-only buttons (expand chevrons, kebab menus, view toggles)
findings.observation: Automated assertion detected 50 buttons without accessible names. Expand chevrons, kebab menus, sort toggles, and view toggle buttons lack aria-label. Fails WCAG 4.1.2 — blocks VPAT certification.
findings.suggestion: Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons: expand ('Expand CVE-2024-3094'), kebab ('Actions for CVE-2024-3094'), view toggle ('Switch to grid view'), sort ('Sort by CVSS ascending').
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Links and Buttons; accessibility.md > Images and Icons

findings.observation: Automated assertion detected 4 form inputs without programmatic labels. Search uses placeholder as only label. Filter dropdowns lack <label> associations. Placeholders are not labels per WCAG.
findings.suggestion: Add visually-hidden <label> elements with for/id associations, or aria-label attributes to all filter inputs.

findings.observation: Many CVE rows show em dashes in CVSS and VECTOR columns. A vulnerability table where most entries lack the primary triage metric (CVSS) undermines the page's value proposition. Looks like incomplete data ingestion.
findings.suggestion: Show 'Awaiting NVD' or 'Pending' with muted style instead of bare em dash. Investigate feed sync completeness. Sort scored CVEs above unscored by default.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; enterprise.md > Scale Appearance

findings.observation: ENDPOINTS column shows '0' for nearly all CVEs. Column header says 'ENDPOINTS' — ambiguous (affected? patched? total?). With 1 enrolled endpoint, technically correct but makes page look disconnected from environment.
findings.suggestion: Rename to 'AFFECTED ENDPOINTS'. Show '--' when correlation hasn't run (vs '0' = scanned, none affected). Highlight rows with endpoints > 0 to draw attention to actionable CVEs.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; enterprise.md > Scale Appearance

findings.element: Duplicate filter mechanisms — stat cards AND severity pills
findings.observation: Two rows serve the same filtering purpose: stat cards (Total/Critical/High/Medium/KEV) and severity pills (All/Critical/High/Medium/Low). Both clickable, both filter the table. Wastes vertical space and creates confusion.
findings.suggestion: Choose one: keep stat cards as non-interactive summary + pill row for filtering, OR remove pill row and make stat cards the sole filter mechanism.
findings.standard_violated: consistency-override > Stat Cards (defines ONE mechanism, not two)

findings.element: CVEs listing — no bulk selection mode
findings.observation: No 'Select' button or checkbox column visible. Security analysts need to select multiple CVEs for bulk deploy, export, or acknowledge. This is a critical triage workflow gap.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Select' button that activates checkboxes + header select-all + bulk action toolbar (Deploy Fix, Export, Acknowledge) with count indicator.
findings.standard_violated: consistency-override > Table Standards > Selection Mode; end-user perspective

findings.element: Deploy Fix workflow path on detail page
findings.observation: Deploy Fix button exists on detail page but workflow path is opaque. Remediation tab shows generic boilerplate recommendations ('Restrict access', 'Review firewall rules') not specific to the CVE. No clear connection from CVE discovery to patch deployment.
findings.suggestion: Make Deploy Fix open deployment wizard pre-populated with relevant patch + affected endpoints. On Remediation tab, add CVE-specific remediation steps and link to actual patch deployment.

findings.observation: CVSS scores shown as bare numbers ('10.0', '8.1') without '/10' denominator in the table. Detail page correctly shows '8.1 / 10'. Inconsistent format.
findings.suggestion: Display CVSS as 'X.X/10' in table consistently. Apply signal colors: >= 9.0 critical, >= 7.0 warning, < 4.0 healthy.

findings.observation: Ring gauge for CVE-2025-1972 (CVSS 8.1 High) uses amber/warning color. Per standards, CVSS >= 7.0 should use --signal-critical (red). Amber understates the severity visually.
findings.suggestion: Use --signal-critical for CVSS >= 9.0, --signal-warning for 7.0-8.9, per CVSS severity breakpoints.

findings.observation: Expanded row uses 2-column layout instead of spec'd 3-column grid. Description text may be truncated without 'show more'. Missing monospace uppercase section labels.
findings.suggestion: Restructure to 3-column grid: (1) Description, (2) Threat Details, (3) Impact/Actions. Add 10px mono uppercase section labels.

findings.element: Kebab menu on CVE table rows
findings.observation: Kebab shows only 'View Details' and 'Deploy Fix' — thin on options. No icons on menu items. 'View Details' duplicates row click. Missing: Copy CVE ID, Open in NVD, Export, Mark as Acknowledged.
findings.suggestion: Add contextual actions: 'Copy CVE ID', 'Open in NVD' (external), 'Export Details'. Add icons to all menu items.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Icon Usage; end-user perspective > Power-user affordances

findings.observation: Published dates show only relative time ('1d ago', '2mo ago') without absolute date tooltip. Security teams need exact publication dates for compliance reporting.
findings.suggestion: Ensure hover tooltip shows full absolute date (e.g., 'Mar 15, 2026 at 2:34 PM UTC'). Consider showing absolute dates by default for CVE publication dates.

findings.observation: ACTION column header exists on Endpoints tab table but cells are empty — no action buttons, links, or icons for any endpoint row.
findings.suggestion: Either add actions ('View Endpoint', 'Deploy Fix') or remove the empty ACTION column.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; interaction.md > Disabled States

findings.observation: Remediation tab shows boilerplate recommendations ('Restrict access to affected components', 'Review firewall rules') not specific to CVE-2025-1972 (binutils buffer overflow). Undermines product credibility.
findings.suggestion: Generate CVE-specific remediation steps (e.g., 'Update binutils to version 2.42-4ubuntu2.10 or later') or remove generic section and focus on patch deployment status.

findings.observation: Intelligence tab shows minimal data: Impact Details, Threat Description (one line), Related CVEs. Missing: EPSS score, exploit-DB links, CISA deadline, CWE classification. Feels placeholder-like for a tab labeled 'Intelligence'.
findings.suggestion: Add EPSS probability, CWE classification, external reference links (NVD, MITRE), CISA deadline (for KEV). Or merge sparse content into Overview tab.

findings.element: Terminology: 'Create Fix' vs 'Deploy Fix' vs 'Create Deployment'
findings.observation: Expanded row shows 'Create Fix', kebab shows 'Deploy Fix', page header has 'Create Deployment'. Three different labels for the same action across the page.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on 'Deploy Fix' everywhere. One concept, one word.

findings.observation: Overview tab shows '0 services' under AFFECTED SERVICES, but summary bar shows 'ENDPOINTS 1 affected' and Endpoints tab shows 1 endpoint. Terminology mismatch + count mismatch.
findings.suggestion: Use 'AFFECTED ENDPOINTS' consistently. Show correct count (1, not 0).

findings.element: Detail page — no EPSS, CWE, or external reference links
findings.observation: CVE detail is missing EPSS score, CWE classification, and external links to NVD/MITRE/vendor advisories. Security teams expect these standard fields from any CVE viewer.
findings.suggestion: Add EPSS score with percentile, CWE type, and clickable external reference links (NVD, MITRE, vendor advisory).
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Compliance Data Display; product-manager perspective

findings.observation: At 768px, filter bar is cramped with truncated controls, sidebar takes horizontal space. Table readable but degraded experience.
findings.suggestion: Collapse filter pills into dropdown or stack filter bar into two rows below 1024px. Collapse sidebar to icon-only mode.

findings.observation: Pagination footer shows page numbers but no per-page size selector (25/50/100). Standard requires 'per-page selector'.
findings.suggestion: Add per-page size dropdown (25/50/100) to pagination footer.

findings.observation: VECTOR column shows em dashes for many rows without explaining why data is missing. No context for whether vector is 'not assessed' vs 'not applicable'.
findings.suggestion: Show 'Awaiting' or 'Not Scored' instead of bare em dash. Or hide column when >50% cells are empty.

findings.observation: Console warns '3 form field elements without id or name attribute'. Affects form serialization and a11y tooling.
findings.suggestion: Add id and name attributes to all form inputs (search, filter dropdowns).

findings.element: Remediation progress bar — accent color instead of signal color
findings.observation: Remediation tab 100% progress bar uses accent color (teal). If accent were changed to ruby, the success bar would turn red — semantically wrong.
findings.suggestion: Use --signal-healthy for 100% remediation, --signal-warning for partial, --signal-critical for very low.
findings.standard_violated: visual-override > Signal Colors; visual-override > Signal/Accent Confusion

findings.element: Exploit/KEV filter buttons — no count badges
findings.observation: Exploit Available and KEV Only toggle buttons highlight on click but don't show counts. Severity pills show counts (Critical 6, High 14) but these binary filters don't.
findings.suggestion: Add count badges: 'Exploit Available (12)', 'KEV Only (11)'.

findings.observation: Filter label 'Has Remediation' is ambiguous — could mean 'has patch available', 'has been remediated', or 'has remediation steps documented'.
findings.suggestion: Rename to 'Patch Available' (if binary) or 'Remediation Status' with clear options: Patch Available / No Patch / Fully Remediated.

findings.observation: PATCH LINKS column shows counts ('1', '2', '28') as plain text. Not clickable. Analyst seeing '28 patch links' wants to click to see which patches.
findings.suggestion: Make count a link that navigates to filtered patches view or expands inline list.

findings.observation: Stat cards show count-first ('6 CRITICAL'), pills show label-first ('Critical 6'). Minor inconsistency within the same page.
findings.suggestion: Standardize ordering. Stat cards: count-first is correct per anatomy spec. Pills: label-first is natural for filter chips. Both are acceptable but visually noisy together.

findings.element: Detail page threat assessment — no source attribution
findings.observation: Threat Assessment section shows metrics (Impact Score, Exploitability, Threat Level) without citing data source or methodology.
findings.suggestion: Add source attribution: 'Source: NVD / CVSS v3.1' or 'Source: PatchIQ Analysis'.

findings.observation: Lighthouse reports SEO score of 60. Low score typically indicates missing meta description and viewport issues. Affects link previews when URLs are shared.
findings.suggestion: Add meta description, ensure viewport tag is correct.

findings.observation: No visible keyboard shortcuts for CVE triage. Analysts triaging 62+ CVEs would benefit from J/K navigation, E to expand, / to search.
findings.suggestion: Add keyboard shortcut overlay (accessible via '?' key).

findings.element: No primary action button in page header
findings.observation: CVEs listing has no primary action button. Patches has 'Create Deployment'. CVEs could have 'Sync CVE Feed' or 'Run CVE Scan' for consistency.
findings.suggestion: Add contextually appropriate primary action or Export button in page header.
